news_feed.text: Tensions escalate near the Strait of Hormuz as naval forces seize a tanker, raising fears of disruption to Gulf crude flows bound for Asian refiners.
Houthi forces claim a fresh missile strike on a crude carrier in the southern Red Sea, prompting more owners to reroute cargoes around Africa.
Saudi Arabia signals it will hold official selling prices steady for Asian buyers next month, easing supply concerns for Indian refiners.
Insurance war-risk premiums for voyages via the Cape of Good Hope tick lower as more operators favour the longer but safer diversion.
A quarterly earnings note discusses refining margins broadly but names no specific corridor, region, or crude grade.

sanctions_feed.text: New sanctions designations target additional vessels lifting Russian Urals crude, tightening compliant tonnage available to importers.
The bloc expands the price-cap enforcement mechanism on seaborne Russian crude, adding paperwork burdens for buyers in Asia.
Iraq's SOMO reassures term customers that Basra Light liftings remain unaffected by regional sanctions discussions.

shipping_feed.text: AIS tracking shows a cluster of VLCCs idling east of the Strait of Hormuz awaiting convoy escort, lengthening effective transit times.
Port congestion at UAE loading terminals eases as a backlog of tankers clears following weather delays.
Diversions around the Red Sea push West African-to-India voyage days higher, squeezing available Suezmax capacity.
US Gulf Coast crude exports hit a seasonal high as arbitrage to Asia stays open despite longer Cape routing.
